Container.Name: MediaForge
Container.Overview: MediaForge is a self-hosted media ingest engine — a single application and single Docker container that handles the complete journey of a raw video file from arrival to library-ready:[br] [b]Ingest[/b] - watch a folder, detect new files, verify they are fully written[br] [b]Identify[/b] - codec detection via ffprobe, metadata lookup via TVDB/TMDB/OMDb, LLM-assisted verification for ambiguous matches[br][b]Transcode[/b] - AVC (H.264) files are queued and encoded to HEVC (H.265) using hardware acceleration (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, or CPU fallback)[br][b]Organize[/b] - rename and move to library with media-server-compatible folder and file naming[br] [br][br]MediaForge is not a media server. It is designed to feed Plex, Jellyfin, Emby, or any other media server that watches a library folder.[br][br][b]GPU Setup (Recommended):[/b][br]Without GPU passthrough, MediaForge falls back to slower CPU encoding.[br][br][b]Intel/AMD:[/b] Add --device=/dev/dri to Extra Parameters.[br][br][b]NVIDIA:[/b] Install the Nvidia-Driver plugin (reboot after) and add --runtime=nvidia to Extra Parameters.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: transcode ffmpeg compression video hevc av1 shrink encode
Container.Requires: For hardware acceleration, GPU passthrough is required. See Overview for setup instructions.

Config.Name: Incoming
Config.Description: Watch folder — drop new media files here for processing

Config.Name: Staging
Config.Description: Fast storage for AVC files awaiting encode and transcode working directory. SSD or cache pool strongly recommended.

Config.Name: Movies Library
Config.Description: Movie library output folder. Can be a locally mounted network share.

Config.Name: TV Library
Config.Description: TV show library output folder. Can be a locally mounted network share.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions. For GPU encoding, set this to the group that owns /dev/dri/renderD128 on your host.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
Config.Description: Exposes NVIDIA GPUs to the container. Default 'all' works for single-GPU systems. For multi-GPU, set to a specific GPU UUID from the Nvidia-Driver plugin page.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
Config.Description: NVIDIA driver features exposed to the container.
